stem: An engineer takes 15 measurements of fuel level using a fuel gauge. The measurements have a mean of 126.1 % and a standard deviation of 13.3 %. However, calibration tests reveal that a fuel gauge systematically overestimates the true value by between 2.0% and 6.6%. Estimate the true fuel level and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 126.1 %. After correcting for the 4.3% bias: ≈ 120.9 %. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (2.0%-6.6%) and sampling variability: [111.4, 130.5]. Point estimate: 120.9 %.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
